sections.section_name: Model Details: Person or organization developing model
Model Details: Paper or other resource for more information

sections.incorrect_information: The “Commercial Use” bullet simplifies Section 2 of the license. The license requires a separate license only if, on the Meta Llama 3 version release date, the monthly active users of the Licensee’s (or affiliates’) products/services exceeded 700M in the preceding calendar month. The model card statement omits this timing and scope nuance, implying a general ongoing threshold.

The statement infers intended user categories (“developers, researchers, and businesses”). The provided files only define “Licensee” broadly as any person or entity; they do not specify these user categories.

sections.section_name: Memory or Hardware Requirements: Loading Requirements
sections.incorrect_information: The claim that at least 16.06 GB of RAM/VRAM is required to load the model is an inference not stated in the repository files and may not account for framework/runtime overhead. Additionally, the citation misnames the file as “model.safensors.index.json.txt”; the correct file is model.safetensors.index.json.

sections.section_name: Memory or Hardware Requirements: Deploying Requirements
Memory or Hardware Requirements: Training or Fine-tuning Requirements

sections.incorrect_information: Misstatement of AUP: The AUP prohibits failing to appropriately disclose known dangers to end users (“You agree you will not … Fail to appropriately disclose …”). The model card says the AUP “requires users to ‘fail to appropriately disclose…’,” which reverses the meaning.

The statement that the prohibition on representing outputs as human-generated implies the model can produce inaccurate or biased content is speculative and not supported by the provided files. The AUP’s prohibition targets deception, not accuracy claims.
